evidence: Reconnect orchestration is split across `RealtimeConnection.handleSubscribeFailure()` (`src/shared/realtime/RealtimeConnection.ts`) and `ReconnectScheduler.requestReconnect()` (`src/integrations/supabase/reconnect/ReconnectScheduler.ts`), with both a direct local retry timer and an event-driven reconnect path.

`RealtimeConnection.handleSubscribeFailure()` schedules `scheduleReconnect(projectId, delay)` and also calls `requestRealtimeReconnect(...)`; `handleAuthHeal()` then calls `doConnect(...)` without clearing pending reconnect timers, creating concurrent reconnect attempt pressure.

Supabase runtime access is globally imported from many domain/shared modules via `getSupabaseClient as supabase` (`src/**` widespread usage), while initialization is centralized in `initializeSupabase()` during app bootstrap (`src/app/bootstrap.tsx`), coupling broad runtime behavior to boot order.

`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` enforces a mutable module-level init flag (`hasInitializedRuntimeClient`) plus singleton runtime client access, reinforcing cross-module hidden dependency on prior initialization.

Mechanical signal check: packet path `holistic_context.coupling.boundary_violations` is absent/null, and `holistic_context.dependencies.deferred_import_density` is absent/null in this blind packet, so those specific detector outputs cannot be confirmed from packet data.

Mechanical signal check: `holistic_context.coupling.module_level_io` entries around `open/onOpenChange` in dialog contracts are mostly naming collisions (UI state shape) rather than import-time I/O side effects; this particular concern is refuted for the sampled files.

Open-state boundary contracts are duplicated across layers with near-identical shape: `OpenStateContract` (`src/shared/components/dialogs/contracts.ts`), `OverlayVisibilityState` (`src/shared/components/overlay/contracts.ts`), and `DeleteGenerationConfirmContract` (`src/domains/generation/contracts/deleteGenerationConfirm.ts` partially redefines the same seam).

Multiple dialog components define local `open/onOpenChange` props instead of composing a single shared contract (`src/shared/components/dialogs/ConfirmDialog.tsx`, `src/shared/components/ModalContainer.tsx`, plus domain-specific modal contracts), which increases seam drift risk.

The architecture still shows compatibility/facade residue around Supabase boundary governance (`src/integrations/supabase/legacy/legacySupabasePolicy.ts`) while most runtime callers directly depend on the facade import path, indicating decomposition goals are documented but not fully realized in structure.
Sibling desktop/mobile implementations of the same ShotImageManager action diverge: desktop duplicates with explicit `timeline_frame`, mobile falls back to `index` when `timeline_frame` is missing.

The mobile path computes `frameNumber = index * batchVideoFrames` in `ShotImageManagerMobile.tsx` but does not use it for duplication, while `ImageGrid.tsx` explicitly documents using real timeline frame for this action.
Holistic naming-drift outliers in this batch were mostly weak signal (barrels/constants flagged for missing shared tokens), but behavioral protocol drift between sibling modules is confirmed.

`submitSegmentTask.ts` mixes hard-fail and soft-fail behavior in one orchestration path: save-settings/create-task throw, but prompt-enhancement metadata fetch/update failures are normalized then silently continued.
`optimisticCacheUpdate.ts` catches all errors and suppresses user feedback (`showToast: false`) with no return channel, creating invisible failures at a data-boundary seam.
`storedPromptCount.ts` swallows storage errors and returns `parseInt` output without finite validation, allowing invalid persisted values to propagate as non-actionable state.

Processing-task policy predicates are inconsistent across seam boundaries: `isProcessingStatusFilter` is true for either queued or in-progress, but `shouldForceProcessingRefetch` only activates when both are selected.
This mismatch affects integration between `paginatedTaskRepository`, `taskFetchPolicy`, and `useProcessingRefetchGuard`, creating stale-empty blind spots for single-status processing filters.
Holistic `exception_hotspots` was null in this packet, so error-seam conclusions were validated by direct code-path inspection rather than hotspot aggregation.

Mechanical indirection hotspot is confirmed: `MediaGalleryLightbox` decomposes props into 9 contract interfaces, then reassembles them into an adapter object (`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/components/MediaGalleryLightbox.tsx`:16-94, 280-345).

`MediaLightboxInputAdapter` is a pure pass-through abstraction (`toMediaLightboxProps` just object-spreads groups into `MediaLightboxProps`), adding another layer with no validation/translation (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/MediaLightboxInputAdapter.tsx`:102-112).

Wide param-bag signal is confirmed in travel task code: multiple modules independently manage large fallback surfaces for the same payload schema (`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts`, `src/shared/lib/taskParamsUtils.ts`).
No delegation-heavy classes/facade modules/TypedDict violations were found in this scope; those mechanical concerns are refuted for reviewed files.
No import cycle was confirmed in reviewed hotspots (including MediaLightbox/MediaGallery and travel task modules).

Dependency direction drift exists inside the codebase structure: generic utility `taskParamsUtils` depends directly on travel-specific readers/snapshots, coupling broad consumers to one task family (`src/shared/lib/taskParamsUtils.ts`:7-8).
Task schema handling is distributed across multiple modules rather than a single canonical parser, increasing internal dependency surface between UI/task-detail code and travel-task internals.
dependency_health.issues_preventing_higher_score: Internal module coupling around task-params parsing remains high even though direct package-level dependency issues/cycles were not confirmed.

evidence: Mechanical scan evidence is confirmed: `complexity_hotspots` flags `useShotEditorController.ts` (846 LOC, complexity 18) and `TimelineContainer.tsx` (784 LOC), both in this subsystem.

`useShotEditorController.ts` mixes navigation, persistence hooks, context fan-in, and UI orchestration in one hook, with many compatibility comments and aliasing branches, making local reasoning expensive.
`payloadBuilder.ts` duplicates legacy-field detection flow (collect + recompute + throw path), creating local indirection and drift risk with `legacyStructureVideo.ts` rules.

`src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/referenceUpload/referenceDomainService.test.ts` defines extensive mocks (file conversion, uploader, resolution, processor, thumbnailer, supabase, cache extraction) but only three failure-path tests; no success-path contract test for upload+thumbnail+persist flow.

Coverage around delete-confirm orchestration is indirect only (`useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm` is mocked in gallery tests), so open/close cancel behavior and confirm handoff contract are not validated at hook/component seam.

Confirmed `holistic_context.api_surface.sync_async_mix` signal for this batch: affected modules include `generationTaskRepository`, `generationTaskCache`, `timelineMutationService`, `toolSettingsService`, `videoUploader`, and join-clips services.

`src/shared/lib/videoUploader.ts` exposes overloaded public signatures where legacy `projectId`/`shotId` arguments are accepted but explicitly unused, creating a contract that implies routing/scoping semantics the implementation no longer honors.

`src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts` exports several symbols flagged as dead exports in packet and not referenced by other source files (`normalizeScopedGenerationInput`, `applyParentTaskFilter`, and multiple exported scope types), while consumers define similar local contracts (e.g. scoped input in `useGenerationMutations`), indicating drifted public surface.

`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/defaults.ts` ships scaffold-like defaults (`model_name: "base_tester_model"`, `debug: true`, `main_output_dir_for_run: "./outputs/default_travel_output"`) that are consumed in `payloadBuilder.ts` when callers omit values.
Comment density and restating prose in migration-heavy modules (for example `useStructureVideo.ts`, `state/types.ts`) is high relative to behavior, increasing ceremony without adding decision logic.

Mechanical evidence was partially confirmed: `systemic_patterns` reports project-wide `workaround_tag`/migration smell families; signal-density hotspots were not present in the exact seed files, so that detector was refuted for this narrow batch scope.

`types.ts` declares canonical payloads should exclude migration aliases, but `uiTypes.ts`, `TimelineMediaContext.tsx`, `useStructureVideo.ts`, and `taskDataService.ts` still carry/read legacy single-video fields in active editor paths.

`payloadBuilder.ts` hard-rejects implicit legacy structure fields in canonical mode, while adjacent read/adaptation code still prioritizes mixed legacy and modern keys, leaving dual contracts alive across write/read boundaries.
Deprecated markers are active across the same flow (`legacyStructureVideo.ts` + enforcement hooks), indicating migration remains in-progress rather than bounded to one adapter boundary.

Structural profile shows `src/shared/components/` at 50 production files and 11,753 LOC with mixed artifact types (`*.tsx` UI plus `segmentSettingsMigration.ts`, `segmentSettingsMigrationLegacy.ts`, `segmentSettingsUtils.ts`, and `useImageGenerationDetailsViewModel.ts`).

`src/shared/hooks/segments/useSegmentSettings.ts:33-34` and `useSegmentMutations.ts:19-20` import domain logic from `@/shared/components/segmentSettings*`, creating non-UI -> UI-directory dependency inversion.

`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideoService.ts:27` imports `buildMotionTaskFields` from `@/shared/components/segmentSettingsUtils`, so a service layer depends on a components folder.

Structural profile shows `src/shared/lib/` at 67 files and 8,595 LOC; `generationTaskBridge.ts` inside this folder exports a React hook (`useGetPrimaryTaskIdForGeneration`) and directly uses `useMutation`, blending hook/UI concerns into a utility package.

Packet field required by prompt is absent in this run: `holistic_context.scan_evidence.mutable_globals` and `holistic_context.errors.mutable_globals` are not present in the blind packet (only `scan_evidence.{complexity_hotspots,dead_code,duplicate_clusters,large_file_distribution,signal_density,systemic_patterns}` and `errors.strategy_by_directory`).
Module-init coupling exists in settings pipeline: `src/shared/hooks/useToolSettings.ts` mutates global queue wiring at import time via `setSettingsWriteFunction(rawUpdateToolSettings)` (line 136).

Global mutable state and import-time side effects are centralized in `src/shared/lib/settingsWriteQueue.ts` (`pendingByTarget`, `flushQueue`, `inFlightCount`, `writeFunction`) with immediate browser event registration at module load (lines 196-205).

Bootstrap-order dependency remains explicit in Supabase runtime: `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` requires `initializeSupabase()` before any `getSupabaseClient()` access and throws otherwise; debug setup (`initializeSupabaseDebugGlobals`) calls `getSupabaseClient()` and writes window globals.

State fields such as `__LAST_AUTH_HEAL_AT__` in `AuthStateManager` use internal-style naming while adjacent API names are conventional public hook/service names.

evidence: Mechanical signal `high_cyclomatic_complexity` is confirmed in reviewed hotspots (`useAppExternalDrop.ts`, `useGenerationMutations.ts`, `generationParamsMapper.ts`, `derivedItemsRepository.ts`, `generationMetadata.ts`).
`useGalleryPageState.ts` mixes pagination, filtering, mutation orchestration, and UI side effects in one hook, increasing mental load.
logic_clarity.issues_preventing_higher_score: Several hooks still combine orchestration and domain mutation logic instead of delegating to smaller flow units.

evidence: `createSupabaseClient.ts` mutates client internals through `as unknown as { rest: { fetch: ... } }` and an explicit `eslint-disable` for `no-explicit-any` behavior.
`generationParamsMapper.ts` and `derivedItemsRepository.ts` repeatedly coerce unknown payloads into broad records (`Record<string, unknown>`) and typed payloads without schema validation.
`useGenerationMutations.ts` `createGeneration` performs two writes (`generations` then `generation_variants`) without transactional rollback; partial success leaves contract-visible orphan records.
`createSupabaseClient.ts` depends on internal `client.rest.fetch` shape that is not part of a stable public contract for long-term compatibility.
Auth checks are generally centralized through `requireSession`/`requireUserFromSession` in reviewed feature hooks.
Protected route handling is consistently enforced through `Layout` auth redirect for tool routes.
authorization_consistency.issues_preventing_higher_score: Some auth assumptions still rely on runtime-initialized global client access instead of explicit dependency injection.

findings.summary: Realtime reconnect is orchestrated by two paths, creating race-prone concurrent reconnect attempts.
findings.suggestion: Choose one reconnect orchestrator as source of truth. Either: (1) keep retries entirely in `RealtimeConnection` and remove scheduler-triggered reconnect for subscribe failures, or (2) move retry scheduling fully into `ReconnectScheduler` and make `RealtimeConnection` react only to scheduler events after clearing pending local timers. Add a single-flight guard (connection attempt token) to reject stale concurrent reconnect attempts.
evidence: `handleSubscribeFailure()` in `RealtimeConnection` both schedules local retry (`scheduleReconnect`) and emits a scheduler request (`requestRealtimeReconnect`).
`handleAuthHeal()` immediately calls `doConnect()` when state is `reconnecting/failed`, but does not clear existing reconnect timers first.
Scheduler coalescing/debounce does not prevent `RealtimeConnection` local timer from firing later, so two reconnect attempts can run close together on the same connection state.

findings.summary: Supabase client facade is imported directly across many layers, coupling most modules to bootstrap state.
findings.suggestion: Introduce boundary-specific data gateways (e.g., generation repository, settings repository, realtime adapter) and ban direct `@/integrations/supabase/client` imports outside integration/repository layers via lint rule. Keep bootstrap ownership in app layer, but inject or import repositories that encapsulate Supabase access and initialization expectations.
evidence: `initializeSupabase()` is called once in app bootstrap, while many feature/shared modules directly call `getSupabaseClient` via alias import.
`client.ts` exposes initialization guard and singleton runtime state (`hasInitializedRuntimeClient`) that all consumers implicitly depend on.
The result is a broad hidden dependency direction: domain/hooks/realtime code depends on integration runtime singleton instead of narrow repositories/adapters.

findings.summary: Dialog/overlay open-state contracts are duplicated across domains, blurring ownership of UI boundary types.
findings.suggestion: Create a single canonical visibility contract in one shared package (e.g., `OpenStateContract`), make overlay/dialog contracts extend it, and convert domain-specific modal contracts to composition (`OpenStateContract & { onConfirm: ... }`). Then update component props to consume shared aliases rather than redefining locally.
evidence: Multiple files define equivalent `open` + `onOpenChange` contract shapes instead of composing one canonical contract.
Domain contract `DeleteGenerationConfirmContract` redefines the common open-state fields and adds `onConfirm`, rather than extending a shared base.
Inline per-component contract copies (`ConfirmDialogProps`, `ModalContainerProps`) create repeated seams and increase drift risk.

findings.summary: Processing filter semantics diverge between fetch and refetch guard paths.
findings.suggestion: Unify processing-filter detection behind one shared predicate (used by fetch sorting and refetch guard), then add tests for three cases: queued-only, in-progress-only, and both-selected to ensure consistent stale-empty recovery.
evidence: `isProcessingStatusFilter` returns true when either `QUEUED` or `IN_PROGRESS` is present, and `paginatedTaskRepository` uses it to apply special processing-feed behavior.
`shouldForceProcessingRefetch` requires both statuses to be selected, so stale-empty recovery is skipped for single-status processing filters.
The guard in `useProcessingRefetchGuard` directly depends on this stricter predicate, producing inconsistent lifecycle behavior for neighboring query modes.

findings.summary: Mobile duplicate action uses index fallback while desktop uses timeline frame.
findings.suggestion: Extract a shared `resolveDuplicateFrame(image, index, batchVideoFrames)` helper and use it in both desktop and mobile item components; remove raw `index` fallback for duplicate placement unless explicitly intended and documented.
evidence: `ImageGrid.tsx` states duplication should use actual `timeline_frame`, and desktop passes `actualTimelineFrame` into `ShotBatchItemDesktop`.
`ShotBatchItemMobile.tsx` calls `onDuplicate(image.id, image.timeline_frame ?? index)`, introducing an index-based fallback that does not match desktop semantics.
`ShotImageManagerMobile.tsx` computes `frameNumber = index * batchVideoFrames` but the mobile duplicate path does not use it, confirming behavior drift between sibling UI protocols.

findings.summary: Multiple boundary utilities swallow failures without signaling degraded outcomes.
findings.suggestion: Define explicit failure contracts for these boundary helpers (for example `Result<{...}, ErrorCode>` or `{ok:boolean, degraded:boolean}`), propagate them to callers, and only suppress errors when caller has an explicit fallback branch; add finite/range validation for stored prompt count before returning.
evidence: In `submitSegmentTask.ts`, metadata fetch/update failures in `saveEnhancedPromptMetadata` are normalized and returned, while task creation continues; other failures in the same flow throw and abort.
`runOptimisticCacheUpdate` catches all exceptions and suppresses toast/output (`showToast: false`) without returning a result to callers, so reconciliation logic cannot react to failure.
`getStoredPromptCount` catches storage exceptions and returns parsed data without `Number.isFinite`/range checks, so invalid persisted values can silently shape prompt state.

findings.summary: Lightbox contracts are split/recombined through pass-through layers with minimal leverage
findings.suggestion: Collapse to one boundary contract: keep grouping only at the call site, remove `MediaLightboxInputAdapter`, and let `MediaLightbox` accept either a single typed `props` object or a small number of meaningful sub-objects that perform actual translation/validation.
evidence: The gallery lightbox defines 9 contract interfaces, destructures them, then rebuilds another grouped adapter object before rendering.
`toMediaLightboxProps` performs only object spreading from grouped adapter sections into `MediaLightboxProps`, with no semantic normalization or safety checks.

findings.summary: Travel task schema precedence is duplicated across creation, reading, and apply-settings seams
findings.suggestion: Create a single canonical `TravelTaskSnapshotReader` contract (writer+reader pair) used by payload building, task details, and apply-settings. Keep precedence tables in one module and expose typed accessors instead of re-implementing fallback chains per consumer.
evidence: Each module encodes its own fallback precedence for prompts/images/structure fields (`orchestrator_details`, `full_orchestrator_payload`, params arrays, segment overrides).
Field-shape and source-order logic is repeated with local variations, increasing drift risk when schema evolves (new field or renamed legacy alias must be updated in several places).

findings.summary: Travel task detection uses inconsistent contract key and over-broad fallback heuristic
findings.suggestion: Use canonical contract fields first (`task_family`, then `family_contract.task_family`), remove `orchestrator_details` as a family classifier, and add regression tests for non-travel payloads that still include orchestration metadata.
evidence: `isTravelTaskParams` checks `task_family_contract` even though canonical payload field is `family_contract` in the task payload contract.
The same predicate classifies any params with `orchestrator_details` as travel, which can route non-travel families through travel-specific prompt logic.

findings.summary: Generation creation is non-atomic and can persist half-written records on variant insert failure
findings.suggestion: Move create-generation+primary-variant into a single RPC/transaction on Supabase (or add compensating delete rollback if second insert fails), and expose one mutation API that guarantees atomicity.
evidence: `createGeneration` inserts into `generations`, then inserts the primary row in `generation_variants`; on second-step failure it throws but does not compensate the first write.
Downstream variant workflows assume coherent generation/variant state (primary-variant-first read paths in variant/shot hooks), so partial writes create fragile edge states.

findings.summary: Critical coordination paths remain untested while existing tests over-mock failure-only branches.
findings.suggestion: Add focused seam tests: (1) direct hook/component tests for delete-confirm open/cancel/confirm transitions, (2) contract tests for settings ID lookup usage, (3) at least one minimally mocked happy-path test for `referenceDomainService` that validates returned URLs/metadata and persistence updates.
evidence: Packet still lists these as `critical_untested` and repo has no direct test files for delete-confirm hook/dialog or settings ID contract module.

`referenceDomainService.test.ts` has heavy module mocking and only failure-path assertions (`reference_aspect_processing_failed`, `reference_thumbnail_auth_required`, `reference_selection_persist_failed`), with no success-path verification of end-to-end contract shape.

findings.summary: Public video upload API keeps legacy parameters that no longer affect behavior.
findings.suggestion: Deprecate legacy overloads behind a separate `uploadVideoToStorageLegacy` wrapper, migrate call sites/tests to the options-object API, then remove unused positional parameters from the primary export to restore interface honesty.
evidence: `uploadVideoToStorage` exposes overloads with `(file, projectId, shotId, ...)` while implementation comment states those params are retained only for backwards compatibility and no longer used in storage path decisions.
Tests still validate legacy signatures, keeping the stale contract alive even though current production callers primarily use options-object form.

findings.summary: Serialized timeline queue has no built-in lease/timeout to guarantee forward progress.
findings.suggestion: Add queue-level guardrails in `timelineWriteQueue`: enforce optional max lease duration per queued task, emit timeout/fault telemetry with requestId+operation, and fail/release the queue deterministically when lease is exceeded. Then require callers to provide operation timeout policy at enqueue time.
evidence: `runSerializedTimelineWrite` queues operations per shot but accepts arbitrary async tasks and does not enforce timeout at queue boundary.
Callers pass multi-step operations through this seam; if any nested step forgets/escapes timeout handling, queued writes behind it can starve indefinitely.

findings.summary: Travel structure migration still runs dual legacy+canonical contracts across core UI and payload paths.
findings.suggestion: Move all legacy shape handling into a single boundary adapter (load-time migration + one serializer), then remove legacy fields from UI/context types and hooks so only canonical `structure_guidance` + `structure_videos` flows through editor internals.
evidence: `TravelBetweenImagesRequestPayload` is documented as legacy-free, but UI state types explicitly reintroduce `LegacyStructureVideoFields`.
Timeline context still exposes `primaryStructureVideo*` legacy single-video props alongside new `structureVideos` array.
`useStructureVideo` persists array format but retains migration code for legacy snake_case/camelCase single-video settings in normal runtime path.

findings.summary: Canonical write path and legacy read path are asymmetric, leaving migration behavior hard to reason about.
findings.suggestion: Define one explicit migration policy matrix (read old/write new only, with end-date), enforce it in shared adapter utilities, and remove per-callsite key precedence logic from `taskDataService` and `payloadBuilder`.
evidence: `payloadBuilder` throws on implicit legacy structure fields in canonical mode via `assertNoImplicitLegacyStructureFields`.
`taskDataService.extractSettings` still reads many legacy keys (`structure_video_path`, `structure_video_type`, `structure_type`) with mixed precedence.
Both paths run policy warning logic, but strictness differs, so migration expectations vary by direction.

findings.summary: Travel payload defaults still include test/scaffold values that leak into runtime behavior.
findings.suggestion: Replace scaffold literals with environment/config-driven production-safe defaults (or require explicit caller values for model/debug/output path), then update tests to assert policy constraints instead of fixed placeholder strings.
evidence: Defaults include `model_name: "base_tester_model"`, `debug: true`, and `main_output_dir_for_run: "./outputs/default_travel_output"`.
`payloadBuilder` uses these defaults when params are absent, so scaffold values are not test-only.
Tests lock these literals in place, making cleanup harder and normalizing non-production placeholders.

findings.summary: Core editor orchestration hooks/components have grown into hard-to-change local god units.
findings.suggestion: Split orchestration by concern: extract dedicated domain controllers (timeline state, generation actions, structure-video migration, navigation) behind narrow interfaces, and keep top-level components/hooks as composition shells.
evidence: `useShotEditorController.ts` is 846 LOC and `TimelineContainer.tsx` is 784 LOC (also surfaced by complexity hotspots), combining many responsibilities in single units.
Controller code mixes persistence wiring, navigation, mutation coordination, and UI-state orchestration, which raises change blast radius for small feature edits.
`payloadBuilder` contains layered conditionals and duplicated legacy checks, adding local branching and maintenance cost.

findings.summary: Non-UI segment settings domain logic is stored under `shared/components`, inverting package boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) Create `src/domains/segment-settings/{model,migration,taskParams}` and move `segmentSettingsUtils.ts`, `segmentSettingsMigration.ts`, `segmentSettingsMigrationLegacy.ts` there first. 2) Update imports in hooks/services (`shared/hooks/segments/*`, `tools/travel-between-images/*`, `shared/components/SegmentSettingsForm/*`) to the new domain package. 3) Move `useImageGenerationDetailsViewModel.ts` into `src/shared/components/ImageGenerationDetails/hooks/` or a domain folder if reused across features. 4) Validate with `npm run lint`, `npm run test -- src/shared/components/__tests__/segmentSettingsMigration.test.ts src/shared/components/__tests__/segmentSettingsUtils.test.ts`, and `npm run test:arch`.
evidence: `segmentSettingsUtils.ts` and `segmentSettingsMigration.ts` implement task-param/migration logic, not rendering concerns.
`useSegmentSettings.ts:33-34` and `useSegmentMutations.ts:19-20` import these files from a components path.
`generateVideoService.ts:27` imports task-field builders from `@/shared/components/segmentSettingsUtils`.

findings.summary: `shared/lib` contains hook-based orchestration (`generationTaskBridge`) that belongs in a hooks/query layer.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) Keep DB/query semantics in `src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts` (or move to `src/domains/generation/repository/` if used beyond shared). 2) Move `useGetPrimaryTaskIdForGeneration` to `src/shared/hooks/tasks/usePrimaryTaskMapping.ts` and re-export temporarily from old path for compatibility. 3) Migrate imports via `rg -l "generationTaskBridge" src | xargs` and switch call sites incrementally, then remove shim. 4) Validate with `npm run lint`, `npm run test -- src/shared/lib/__tests__/generationTaskBridge.test.ts src/shared/components/TaskDetails/hooks/__tests__/useGenerationTaskDetails.test.ts`, and `npm run test:arch`.
evidence: `generationTaskBridge.ts:1,17-24` imports `useMutation` and exports `useGetPrimaryTaskIdForGeneration`, which is React-hook behavior in a `lib` package.
Consumers like `useGenerationTaskDetails.ts:5` import hook API from `@/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge`, coupling package semantics (utility vs hook).
Mechanical scan evidence flagged `generationTaskRepository.ts` in signal-density and responsibility-cohesion sets; this bridge/repository split currently straddles unclear boundaries.

findings.summary: The `shared/*` namespace has become a broad default placement, obscuring capability ownership.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) Define feature/domain homes (`src/domains/shots/ui/`, `src/domains/resources/ui/`, `src/domains/segment-settings/`) and treat `src/shared` as primitives/platform only. 2) Move domain-heavy modals first (`CreateShotModal`, `DatasetBrowserModal`) with compatibility re-exports under old paths. 3) Move co-owned hooks/services with each feature package and keep only generic primitives in shared. 4) Remove compatibility exports after import migration; verify with `npm run lint`, `npm run build`, and `npm run test:arch` to catch boundary regressions.
evidence: Directory profiles show very large top-level shared packages (`shared/components` 50 files, `shared/hooks` 69, `shared/lib` 67), with wide fan-in from many features/tools.

`CreateShotModal` and `DatasetBrowserModal` encode workflow/domain behavior (project aspect updates, resource ownership/public toggles, Supabase session-driven behavior) while living in global shared UI namespace.
Coupling matrix confirms shared-centralized dependency gravity, increasing ripple-risk when domain workflows evolve.

findings.summary: Supabase access depends on mutable module singletons and strict bootstrap order
findings.suggestion: Replace implicit global bootstrap gates with an explicit client registry/injection boundary: provide Supabase client through app/provider context (or function parameter) to repositories, and make startup idempotent behind one runtime manager with clear readiness contract.
evidence: `client.ts` tracks bootstrap state with `hasInitializedRuntimeClient` and returns errors when `getSupabaseClient()` is called early.
`supabaseRuntime.ts` keeps mutable `runtimeClient` singleton and throws before initialization, so import/use order controls behavior.
Feature repositories call `getSupabaseClient()` directly instead of receiving a client dependency, spreading init-order coupling across data access code.

findings.summary: Editor/lightbox orchestration relies on oversized state-bag contracts
findings.suggestion: Split orchestration by workflow boundaries (navigation, variant state, shot actions, edit-mode state, join workflow) with narrower typed contracts per slice; move pass-through mapping into dedicated adapters near each consumer and keep each controller/hook focused on one change axis.
evidence: `useShotEditorController.ts` aggregates many concerns (settings, generation, join flows, image operations, navigation) and builds very large adapter payloads (`useGenerationController`, `useApplySettingsHandler`, large `layoutProps`).
`ImageLightbox.tsx` constructs broad environment/shared-input objects and then maps them into `useSharedLightboxState`, creating long pass-through contracts.
`useSharedLightboxState.ts` defines large grouped interfaces and a large return surface, indicating responsibility spread rather than cohesive module boundaries.

findings.summary: Join clips hydration effects can apply stale async results after project switches
findings.suggestion: Add a per-project request version/cancellation guard (e.g., incrementing ref token or `AbortController`) and discard stale async completions when project/context changes; isolate hydration pipeline into a dedicated hook/service that returns guarded state transitions.
evidence: `useClipManager.ts` resets state on project change (`setClips([])`, `setInitialHydrationComplete(false)`), but async calls from earlier project context still resolve and call `setClips`/`setInitialHydrationComplete` (pending clip consume and poster preload paths).
The async branches in `consumePendingJoinClips(...).then(...)` and `preloadPosterImages(...).then(...)` do not validate current project/version before mutating state.
This mixes initialization, persistence, and async queue processing in one hook without lifecycle guards, making ordering bugs likely under rapid project switches.

findings.summary: Play-event listeners are added per clip without effective cleanup
findings.suggestion: Collect listener cleanup functions in an array and return one top-level cleanup function from the effect; alternatively attach this behavior at the `SortableClip` element level with proper mount/unmount cleanup per video element.
evidence: In `useClipManager.ts`, the effect that iterates `clips` adds `video.addEventListener('play', preventPlay)` but returns cleanup from inside `forEach`, so React never receives those cleanups.
As clips change, listeners can accumulate on reused video elements, creating hidden behavior coupling and potential performance/logic drift.

findings.summary: Lightbox forces rerenders via query-cache subscription/version bump side channel
findings.suggestion: Replace manual cache subscription with explicit query selectors (`useQuery`/derived hook) keyed to the active generation/task mapping, and propagate optimistic starred updates through typed mutation updaters instead of global cache-event listeners.
evidence: `MediaGalleryLightbox.tsx` subscribes directly to `queryClient.getQueryCache()` and bumps local `cacheVersion` on broad `updated` events.
The memoized media enhancement explicitly references `void cacheVersion` just to force recomputation, which is an implicit rerender trigger rather than explicit data dependency.
This couples component behavior to low-level cache event mechanics and broad query-key matching, making behavior hard to reason about as cache usage evolves.

findings.summary: Lightbox flow is fragmented into repetitive prop-contract translation layers
findings.suggestion: Create a single `LightboxSessionModel` (or context/store) built once at gallery boundary, then pass stable selectors/actions to MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox. Remove repeated contract rebundling and eliminate `void`-based reactivity nudges by making state transitions explicit.
evidence: MediaGalleryLightbox defines many contract interfaces (`core/navigation/actions/shotWorkflow/ui/optimistic/taskModal/taskData/integration`) and manually reassembles them, then forwards to adapter/lightbox.
MediaLightbox immediately re-bundles another flat prop surface via `useBundledLightboxProps`, then re-fans out to Image/Video lightboxes.
useSharedLightboxState introduces another large grouped input schema and orchestration layer, increasing interface choreography instead of reducing it.
`void cacheVersion` in MediaGalleryLightbox memo path is a workaround for this fragmented state ownership.

findings.summary: PhilosophyPane mixes copy, animation CSS, and complex media layout logic in one component
findings.suggestion: Split into section-level presentational components (`TravelDemoSection`, `MotionReferenceSection`, `LoraStyleSection`) and use a shared `TravelVideoCard` renderer driven by config (aspect, refs, poster, callbacks) to remove branch-level markup duplication.
evidence: Component is 627 LOC and contains multiple full UI sections plus inline keyframes and a large inline IIFE layout branch for travel variants.
Nearly identical video/poster/play-overlay blocks are repeated for each variant path (2/4/7 image flows), increasing change risk and drift potential.
Behavioral media logic is already extracted to `usePhilosophyPaneMedia`, but visual composition remains monolithic and heavily duplicated.

findings.summary: StrokeOverlay combines interaction state machine, global lifecycle, rendering, and export IO
findings.suggestion: Split into: (1) pure interaction reducer/service for stroke state transitions, (2) renderer component for Konva primitives, and (3) export utility for mask generation. Keep imperative API thin and delegate behavior to tested pure modules.
evidence: StrokeOverlay file (527 LOC) owns pointer capture/release, drag/resize math, keyboard delete handling, global window listeners, rendering, and offscreen mask export in one module.
The component also exposes imperative mutation/export APIs (`undo`, `clear`, `deleteSelected`, `toggleFreeForm`, `exportMask`) while simultaneously managing rendering concerns.
MediaDisplayWithCanvas passes a wide behavioral prop set into StrokeOverlay, signaling an overgrown interaction contract.

findings.summary: Global singleton wiring relies on import-time side effects and runtime order assumptions
findings.suggestion: Introduce an explicit app bootstrap registry (e.g., `initializeRuntimeServices`) that constructs and wires settings queue, Supabase runtime, and debug adapters in one place; replace module singletons with injected service instances and gate dev globals behind registry-owned diagnostics toggles.
evidence: `useToolSettings.ts` initializes queue behavior via module-level `setSettingsWriteFunction(...)`, creating hidden dependency on import order rather than explicit bootstrapping.
`settingsWriteQueue.ts` keeps mutable module-level state and registers window/document listeners at import time, making lifecycle ownership implicit.
`client.ts` enforces bootstrap ordering (`initializeSupabase()` before `getSupabaseClient()`), while debug/global helpers and reconnect scheduler also expose mutable globals on `window`.

findings.summary: Supabase client internals are monkey-patched through unsafe casts
findings.suggestion: Replace internal patching with a supported extension point (custom `fetch` that conditionally injects auth headers per request context), or wrap data access in a repository adapter that controls token injection without mutating third-party object internals.
evidence: `createSupabaseClient.ts` assigns `(client as unknown as { rest: { fetch: ... } }).rest.fetch = dataFetch` and suppresses type safety with an eslint disable.
This assumes a private internal `rest.fetch` field shape that TypeScript cannot verify against package contract.

findings.summary: Generation creation is non-atomic and can leave orphaned rows
findings.suggestion: Move the two-write flow into a single RPC/transaction in Supabase, or add compensating delete rollback when second step fails and cover failure path with explicit tests.
evidence: `createGeneration` inserts into `generations` and then separately into `generation_variants`.
If variant insert fails, the first insert remains committed; current logic throws but does not compensate/rollback.

findings.summary: Related UI orchestration paths hide failures with inconsistent semantics
findings.suggestion: Standardize these flows on a shared `Result` contract (`ok/error + reason`) so orchestration layers can distinguish hard failure, partial success, and expected no-op states.
evidence: `useAppExternalDrop` catches all errors, emits presentation error, and always finalizes overlay without returning operation status.
`adjacentPagePreloadCoordinator` catches prefetch errors and logs via callback, but callers receive no failure signal for degraded behavior.

findings.summary: Feature barrel exposes shared UI internals as public API
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical image-edit API boundary (`domains/image-edit` types + `features/image-edit` behavior). Keep `shared/components/MediaLightbox` private by importing through feature-owned adapters only.
evidence: `src/features/image-edit/index.ts` re-exports numerous modules from `shared/components/MediaLightbox/*` directly.
A separate `src/domains/imageEdit/index.ts` also defines the same concept domain surface, creating competing API authorities.

findings.summary: Runtime boot relies on mutable module globals and window mutations
findings.suggestion: Introduce a single `AppRuntimeRegistry` responsible for startup phases, singleton ownership, and reversible debug instrumentation; require all initializers to register setup/teardown handles and enforce ordering through explicit phase APIs.
evidence: Boot idempotence is managed by module flags (`environmentInitialized`, `presenterInstalled`, `hasInitializedRuntimeClient`, singleton instances) rather than a centralized runtime registry.

Initialization installs global side effects (`window.supabase`, `window.__supabase_client__`, `window.__RECONNECT_SCHEDULER__`, `window.onerror`, `window.WebSocket`) with no coordinated uninstall lifecycle.

findings.summary: Delete-confirm orchestration hook lacks direct behavior tests
findings.suggestion: Add focused hook tests for `useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm` and `useDeleteGenerationAction` covering request/cancel/confirm lifecycle, missing project-id branch, and mutation settle behavior.
evidence: Packet testing context flags `useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.ts` as critical untested (5 importers).
Existing tests cover underlying mutation hooks but not confirm-dialog state transitions (`pendingDeleteId`, `deletingId`, cancel/confirm coordination).

findings.summary: Legacy Supabase migration remains partially active
findings.suggestion: Close the migration with a staged plan: (1) audit and remove remaining legacy imports, (2) delete legacy policy/constants and facade contract tests, (3) simplify env/client contract to post-migration surface only.
evidence: `env.ts` contains explicit remove-by migration contract for legacy symbols.
Legacy policy module and compatibility tests still enforce transition behavior, indicating migration path remains unresolved.

findings.summary: Package boundaries blur ownership and concentrate cross-domain coupling
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: Stage 1 target folders: create `src/domains/image-edit/{contracts,types}` and `src/features/image-edit/{hooks,components,adapters}`; Stage 2 move order: move domain type exports from `domains/imageEdit` first, then move feature barrel adapters, then make `shared/components/MediaLightbox/*` internal-only behind feature adapters; Stage 3 split `domains/generation/types` into `contracts` vs `internal` and update importers incrementally. After each stage run `rg "domains/imageEdit|shared/components/MediaLightbox" src -n`, `pnpm -s tsc --noEmit`, and `pnpm -s vitest run src/domains/generation src/features/image-edit src/features/gallery` to validate import updates and behavior.
evidence: `domains/generation/types/` has very high fan-in (avg_fan_in 25.3), acting as a broad global type hub rather than bounded domain contract.
`features/gallery/hooks/useGalleryPageState.ts` imports across domains/shared/features and centralizes many concerns, reflecting structural flattening.
Image-edit concept is split between domain and feature folders with implementation anchored in shared UI internals.

findings.summary: Supabase instrumentation modules combine global patching, logging, and diagnostics
findings.suggestion: Split instrumentation into focused units: `globalPatchers` (install/uninstall only), `eventCollectors` (parse/capture), and `diagnosticReporters` (normalize/log); wire them via explicit composition in one small bootstrap module.
evidence: `instrumentation/window` overrides global handlers (`window.onerror`, `window.onunhandledrejection`, `window.WebSocket`) while also owning event parsing and timeline writing.
`instrumentation/realtime` mutates runtime object descriptors and also owns corruption reporting and timeline events.

findings.summary: Supabase API exposes both throwing and non-throwing access paths inconsistently
findings.suggestion: Converge on one explicit contract for app code (prefer non-throwing accessor + centralized bootstrap assert), and reserve throwing accessor for internal bootstrap-only paths.
evidence: `client.ts` offers `getSupabaseClientResult` (safe) and `getSupabaseClient` (throwing), but many consumers directly call throwing accessor.
This creates mixed call-site expectations about whether missing initialization is recoverable vs exceptional.

findings.summary: Gallery hook blends query, filtering, mutation commands, and UI notifications
findings.suggestion: Decompose into `useGalleryQueryState`, `useGalleryActions`, and `useGalleryPagingEffects`; keep each hook returning focused contracts and compose them in a thin page controller.
evidence: `useGalleryPageState.ts` owns pagination state, filter coordination, query fallback behavior, add-to-shot orchestration, and deletion/star actions.
Adjacent preloader exists as separate logic but integration remains ad hoc at hook level, not via narrower service interfaces.
